title: Reading - Part 1
instructions: For each question, choose the correct answer.

questions.heading: After-school Coding
questions.text: Learn to make simple games in our six-week coding course. Computers are provided, but students should bring headphones. The course is not suitable for complete beginners, as basic keyboard skills are needed.
questions.question: Who should join the course?

options.A: Students with some computer experience already.
options.B: Students who have never used a keyboard.
options.C: Students who can bring their own computers.

questions.explanation: Students need basic keyboard skills, so some experience is needed.

questions.heading: Message from Chloe to Ryan
questions.text: Ryan, I’m going to the library after school instead of going straight home. I still want to finish the poster tonight, but I need to borrow a book first. Can we start at six?
questions.question: Why is Chloe texting Ryan?

options.B: To suggest starting their work later.
options.A: To say she has finished the poster.
options.C: To ask him to borrow a book for her.

questions.explanation: Chloe wants to start the poster at six because she is going to the library first.

questions.heading: Park Café
questions.text: The café terrace is open again after repairs. Customers can sit outside, but please do not move tables onto the grass. Staff need to keep the path clear for people with pushchairs.
questions.question: What are customers asked not to do?

options.C: Change where the tables are placed.
options.A: Sit outside on the café terrace.
options.B: Use the path with pushchairs safely.

questions.explanation: Customers should not move tables onto the grass.

questions.heading: From: Mr Foster / Subject: Art homework
questions.text: Several people painted excellent landscapes this week. For homework, choose one part of your painting and describe how you could improve it. Do not start a new picture yet.
questions.question: What should students do for homework?

options.A: Think about improving their existing work.
options.B: Paint another landscape at home instead.
options.C: Write about someone else’s picture instead.

questions.explanation: Students must describe how they could improve part of their current painting.

questions.heading: Local History Walk
questions.text: Join our guided walk around the old town on Sunday. The walk lasts about ninety minutes and ends at the museum. Please wear comfortable shoes, as some streets are steep.
questions.question: What does the poster warn people about?

options.B: The walk may be physically tiring.
options.A: The museum will be closed on Sunday.
options.C: The walk starts at the museum.

questions.explanation: People need comfortable shoes because some streets are steep.
